);\n205\t\n206\t    -- Recovery Sessions (for anomaly/recovery audit trail)\n207\t    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS recovery_sessions (\n208\t        correlation_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n209\t into individual statements, respecting string literals,\n243\t/// quoted identifiers, and comments.\n244\t///\n245\t/// A naive `split(';')` breaks when SQL string literals contain semicolons\n246\t/// (e.g., `INSERT INTO t(v) VALUES('a;b')`). This function uses a small state\n247\t/// machine to track whether the current position is inside:\n248\t/// - A single-quoted string literal (`'...'`, with `''` as escape)\n249\t/// - A double-quoted identifier (`\"...\"`, with `\"\"` as escape)\n250\t/// - A line comment (`-- ...`)\n251\t/// - A block comment (`/* ... */`)\n252\t///\n253\t/// Only semicolons at the top level (outside all of the above) are treated as\n254\t/// statement terminators.\n255\tfn split_sql_statements(sql: &str) -> Vec<&str> {\n256\t    let bytes = sql.as_bytes();\n257\t    let len = bytes.len();\n258\t    let mut stmts = Vec::new();\n259\t    let mut start = 0; // byte offset where the current statement begins\n260\t    let mut i = 0;\n261\t\n262\t    // State flags — at most one is true at a time.\n263\t    let mut in_single_quote = false;\n264\t    let mut in_double_quote = false;\n265\t    let mut in_line_comment = false;\n266\t    let mut in_block_comment = false;\n267\t\n268\t    while i < len {\n269\t        let b = bytes[i];\n270\t\n271\t        // --- Line comment state ---\n272\t        if in_line_comment {\n273\t            if b == b'\\n' {\n274\t                in_line_comment = false;\n275\t            }\n276\t            i += 1;\n277\t            continue;\n278\t        }\n279\t\n280\t        // --- Block comment state ---\n281\t        if in_block_comment {\n282\t            if b == b'*' && i + 1 < len && bytes[i + 1] == b'/' {\n283\t                in_block_comment = false;\n284\t                i += 2;\n285\t            } else {\n286\t                i += 1;\n287\t            }\n288\t            continue;\n289\t        }\n290\t\n291\t        // --- Single-quoted string state ---\n292\t        if in_single_quote {\n293\t            if b == b'\\'' {\n294\t                // '' is an escaped quote inside a string literal\n295\t                if i + 1 < len && bytes[i + 1] == b'\\'' {\n296\t                    i += 2;\n297\t                } else {\n298\t                    in_single_quote = false;\n299\t                    i += 1;\n300\t                }\n301\t            } else {\n302\t                i += 1;\n303\t            }\n304\t            continue;\n305\t        }\n306\t\n307\t        // --- Double-quoted identifier state ---\n308\t        if in_double_quote {\n309\t            if b == b'\"' {\n310\t                if i + 1 < len && bytes[i + 1] == b'\"' {\n311\t                    i += 2;\n312\t                } else {\n313\t                    in_double_quote = false;\n314\t                    i += 1;\n315\t                }\n316\t            } else {\n317\t                i += 1;\n318\t            }\n319\t            continue;\n320\t        }\n321\t\n322\t        // --- Top-level parsing ---\n323\t        if b == b'\\'' {\n324\t            in_single_quote = true;\n325\t            i += 1;\n326\t        } else if b == b'\"' {\n327\t            in_double_quote = true;\n328\t            i += 1;\n329\t        } else if b == b'-' && i + 1 < len && bytes[i + 1] == b'-' {\n330\t            in_line_comment = true;\n331\t            i += 2;\n332\t        } else if b == b'/' && i + 1 < len && bytes[i + 1] == b'*' {\n333\t            in_block_comment = true;\n334\t            i += 2;\n335\t        } else if b == b';' {\n336\t            // Statement terminator at top level.\n337\t            let stmt = &sql[start..i];\n338\t            if !stmt.trim().is_empty() {\n339\t                stmts.push(stmt.trim());\n340\t            }\n341\t            start = i + 1;\n342\t            i += 1;\n343\t        } else {\n344\t            i += 1;\n345\t        }\n346\t    }\n347\t\n348\t    // Trailing statement without a final semicolon.\n349\t    if start < len {\n350\t        let stmt = &sql[start..len];\n351\t        if !stmt.trim().is_empty() {\n352\t            stmts.push(stmt.trim());\n353\t        }\n354\t    }\n355\t\n356\t    stmts\n357\t}\n358\t\n359\t/// Execute multiple SQL statements separated by semicolons.\n360\tfn execute_batch(conn: &Connection, sql: &str) -> anyhow::Result<()> {\n361\t    conn.execute_batch(sql)?;\n362\t    Ok(())\n363\t}\n364\t\n365\tpub fn apply_schema(conn: &Connection) -> anyhow::Result<()> {\n366\t    execute_batch(conn, SCHEMA_SQL)?;\n367\t    Ok(())\n368\t}\n369\t\n370\tpub fn ensure_wal_mode(conn: &Connection) -> anyhow::Result<()> {\n371\t    // Use execute_batch so PRAGMAs that return rows don't cause errors\n372\t    conn.execute_batch(\n373\t        \"PRAGMA journal_mode = WAL;\n374\t         PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;\n375\t         PRAGMA busy_timeout = 30000;\n376\t         PRAGMA cache_size = -8000;\n377\t         PRAGMA synchronous = NORMAL;\"\n378\t    title TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(length(title) <= 500),\n        description TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',\n        design TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',\n        acceptance_criteria TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',\n        notes TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',\n        status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'open',\n        priority INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 2 CHECK(priority >= 0 AND priority <= 4),\n        issue_type TEXT NOT NULL 'conditional-blocks' OR type = 'waits-for');\n\n    -- Labels\n    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS labels (\n        issue_id TEXT NOT NULL,\n        label TEXT NOT NULL,\n        PRIMARY KEY (issue_id,    -- Child Counters (for hierarchical IDs like bd-abc.1, bd-abc.2)\n    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS child_counters (\n        parent_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n        last_child INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT idx_anomaly_audit_correlation ON anomaly_audit(correlation_id);\n    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_anomaly_audit_timestamp ON anomaly_audit(timestamp);\n    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_anomaly_audit_event_type ON anomaly_audit(event_type);\n    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_anomaly_audit_anomaly_type ON anomaly_audit(anomaly_type);\n    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_anomaly_audit_outcome ON anomaly_audit(outcome);\n\";\n\n/// Split a SQL script into individual statements, respecting string literals,\n/// quoted identifiers, and comments.\n///\n/// A naive `split(';')` breaks when SQL string literals contain semicolons\n/// (e.g., `INSERT INTO t(v) VALUES('a;b')`). This function uses a small state\n/// machine to track whether the current position is inside:\n/// - A single-quoted string literal (`'...'`, with `''` as escape)\n/// - A double-quoted identifier (`\"...\"`, with `\"\"` as escape)\n/// - A line comment (`-- ...`)\n/// - A block comment (`/* ... */`)\n///\n/// Only semicolons at the top level (outside all of the above) are treated as\n/// statement terminators.\nfn split_sql_statements(sql: &str) -> Vec<&str> {\n    let bytes = sql.as_bytes();\n    let len = bytes.len();\n    let mut stmts = Vec::new();\n    let mut start = 0; // byte offset where the current statement begins\n    let mut i = 0;\n\n    // State flags — at most one is true at a time.\n    let mut in_single_quote = false;\n    let mut in_double_quote = false;\n    let mut in_line_comment = false;\n    let mut in_block_comment = false;\n\n    while i < len {\n        let b = bytes[i];\n\n        // --- Line comment state ---\n        if in_line_comment {\n            if b == b'\\n' {\n                in_line_comment = false;\n            }\n            i += 1;\n            continue;\n        }\n\n        // --- Block comment state ---\n        if in_block_comment {\n            if b == b'*' && i + 1 < len && bytes[i + 1] == b'/' {\n                in_block_comment = false;\n                i += 2;\n            } else {\n                i += 1;\n            }\n            continue;\n        }\n\n        // --- Single-quoted string state ---\n        if in_single_quote {\n            if b == b'\\'' {\n                // '' is an escaped quote inside a string literal\n                if i + 1 < len && bytes[i + 1] == b'\\'' {\n                    i += 2;\n                } else {\n                    in_single_quote = false;\n                    i += 1;\n                }\n            } else {\n                i += 1;\n            }\n            continue;\n        }\n\n        // --- Double-quoted identifier state ---\n        if in_double_quote {\n            if b == b'\"' {\n                if i + 1 < len && bytes[i + 1] == b'\"' {\n                    i += 2;\n                } else {\n                    in_double_quote = false;\n                    i += 1;\n                }\n            } else {\n                i += 1;\n            }\n            continue;\n        }\n\n        // --- Top-level parsing ---\n        if b == b'\\'' {\n            in_single_quote = true;\n            i += 1;\n        } else if b == b'\"' {\n            in_double_quote = true;\n            i += 1;\n        } else if b == b'-' && i + 1 < len && bytes[i + 1] == b'-' {\n            in_line_comment = true;\n            i += 2;\n        } else if b == b'/' && i + 1 < len && bytes[i + 1] == b'*' {\n            in_block_comment = true;\n            i += 2;\n        } else if b == b';' {\n            // Statement terminator at top level.\n            let stmt = &sql[start..i];\n            if !stmt.trim().is_empty() {\n                stmts.push(stmt.trim());\n            }\n            start = i + 1;\n            i += 1;\n        } else {\n            i += 1;\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Trailing statement without a final semicolon.\n    if start < len {\n        let stmt = &sql[start..len];\n        if !stmt.trim().is_empty() {\n            stmts.push(stmt.trim());\n        }\n    }\n\n    stmts\n}\n\n/// Execute multiple SQL statements separated by semicolons.\nfn execute_batch(conn: &Connection, sql: &str) -> anyhow::Result<()> {\n    conn.execute_batch(sql)?;\n    Ok(())\n}\n\npub fn apply_schema(conn: &Connection) -> anyhow::Result<()> {\n    execute_batch(conn, SCHEMA_SQL)?;\n    Ok(())\n}\n\npub fn ensure_wal_mode(conn: &Connection) -> anyhow::Result<()> {\n    // Use execute_batch so PRAGMAs that return rows don't cause errors\n    conn.execute_batch(\n        \"PRAGMA journal_mode = WAL;\n         PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;\n         PRAGMA busy_timeout = 30000;\n         PRAGMA IDs.\n196\t    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS blocked_issues_cache (\n197\t        issue_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n198\t        blocked_by TEXT NOT NULL,\n199\t        blocked_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,\n200\t        FOREIGN KEY (issue_id) REFERENCES issues(id) ON DELETE CASCADE\n201\t    );\n202\t    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_blocked_cache_blocked_at ON blocked_issues_cache(blocked_at);\n203\t\n204\t    -- Child Counters (for hierarchical IDs like bd-abc.1, bd-abc.2)\n205\t    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS child_counters (\n206\t        parent_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n207\t        last_child INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,\n208\t        FOREIGN KEY (parent_id) REFERENCES issues(id) ON DELETE CASCADE\n209\t    );\n210\t\n211\t    -- Recovery Sessions (for anomaly/recovery audit trail)\n212\t    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS recovery_sessions (\n213\t        correlation_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n214\t        started_at TEXT NOT NULL,\n215\t        completed_at TEXT,\n216\t        final_outcome TEXT,\n217\t        session_type TEXT NOT NULL,\n218\t        trigger_reason TEXT,\n219\t        workspace_path TEXT,\n220\t        summary TEXT\n221\t    );\n222\t    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_recovery_sessions_started ON recovery_sessions(started_at);\n223\t    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_recovery_sessions_outcome ON recovery_sessions(final_outcome);\n224\t    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_recovery_sessions_type ON recovery_sessions(session_type);\n225\t\n226\t    -- Anomaly Audit Records (for anomaly/recovery event tracking)\n227\t    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS anomaly_audit (\n228\t        id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n229\t        correlation_id TEXT NOT NULL,\n230\t        timestamp TEXT NOT NULL,\n231\t        event_type TEXT NOT NULL,\n232\t        anomaly_type TEXT,\n233\t        recovery_strategy TEXT,\n234\t        outcome TEXT,\n235\t        evidence TEXT,\n236\t        metadata TEXT,\n237\t        created_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT (CURRENT_TIMESTAMP),\n238\t        FOREIGN KEY (correlation_id) REFERENCES recovery_sessions(correlation_id) ON DELETE CASCADE\n239\t    );\n240\t    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_anomaly_audit_correlation ON anomaly_audit(correlation_id);\n241\t    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_anomaly_audit_timestamp ON anomaly_audit(timestamp);\n242\t    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_anomaly_audit_event_type ON anomaly_audit(event_type);\n243\t    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_anomaly_audit_anomaly_type ON anomaly_audit(anomaly_type);\n244\t    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_anomaly_audit_outcome ON anomaly_audit(outcome);\n245\t\";\n246\t\n247\t/// Split a SQL script into individual statements, respecting string literals,\n248\t/// quoted identifiers, and comments.\n249\t///\n250\t/// A naive `split(';')` breaks when SQL string literals contain semicolons\n251\t/// (e.g., `INSERT INTO t(v) VALUES('a;b')`). This function uses a small state\n252\t/// machine to track whether the current position is inside:\n253\t/// - A single-quoted string literal (`'...'`, with `''` as escape)\n254\t/// - A double-quoted identifier (`\"...\"`, with `\"\"` as escape)\n255\t/// - A line comment (`-- ...`)\n256\t/// - A block comment (`/* ... */`)\n257\t///\n258\t/// Only semicolons at the top level (outside all of the above) are treated as\n259\t/// statement terminators.\n260\tfn split_sql_statements(sql: &str) -> Vec<&str> {\n261\t    let bytes = sql.as_bytes();\n262\t    let len = bytes.len();\n263\t    let mut stmts = Vec::new();\n264\t    let mut start = 0; // byte offset where the current statement begins\n265\t    let mut i = 0;\n266\t\n267\t    // State flags — at most one is true at a time.\n268\t    let mut in_single_quote = false;\n269\t    let mut in_double_quote = false;\n270\t    let mut in_line_comment = false;\n271\t    let mut in_block_comment = false;\n272\t\n273\t    while i < len {\n274\t        let b = bytes[i];\n275\t\n276\t        // --- Line comment state ---\n277\t        if in_line_comment {\n278\t            if b == b'\\n' {\n279\t                in_line_comment = false;\n280\t            }\n281\t            i += 1;\n282\t            continue;\n283\t        }\n284\t\n285\t        // --- Block comment state ---\n286\t        if in_block_comment {\n287\t            if b == b'*' && i + 1 < len && bytes[i + 1] == b'/' {\n288\t                in_block_comment = false;\n289\t                i += 2;\n290\t            } else {\n291\t                i += 1;\n292\t            }\n293\t            continue;\n294\t        }\n295\t\n296\t        // --- Single-quoted string state ---\n297\t        if in_single_quote {\n298\t            if b == b'\\'' {\n299\t                // '' is an escaped quote inside a string literal\n300\t                if i + 1 < len && bytes[i + 1] == b'\\'' {\n301\t                    i += 2;\n302\t                } else {\n303\t                    in_single_quote = false;\n304\t                    i += 1;\n305\t                }\n306\t            } else {\n307\t                i += 1;\n308\t            }\n309\t            continue;\n310\t        }\n311\t\n312\t        // --- Double-quoted identifier state ---\n313\t        if in_double_quote {\n314\t            if b == b'\"' {\n315\t                if i + 1 < len && bytes[i + 1] == b'\"' {\n316\t                    i += 2;\n317\t                } else {\n318\t                    in_double_quote = false;\n319\t                    i += 1;\n320\t                }\n321\t            } else {\n322\t                i += 1;\n323\t            }\n324\t            continue;\n325\t        }\n326\t\n327\t        // --- Top-level parsing ---\n328\t        if b == b'\\'' {\n329\t            in_single_quote = true;\n330\t            i += 1;\n331\t        } else if b == b'\"' {\n332\t            in_double_quote = true;\n333\t            i += 1;\n334\t        } else if b == b'-' && i + 1 < len && bytes[i + 1] == b'-' {\n335\t            in_line_comment = true;\n336\t            i += 2;\n337\t        } else if b == b'/' && i + 1 < len && bytes[i + 1] == b'*' {\n338\t            in_block_comment = true;\n339\t            i += 2;\n340\t        } else if b == b';' {\n341\t            // Statement terminator at top level.\n342\t            let stmt = &sql[start..i];\n343\t            if !stmt.trim().is_empty() {\n344\t                stmts.push(stmt.trim());\n345\t            }\n346\t            start = i + 1;\n347\t            i += 1;\n348\t        } else {\n349\t            i += 1;\n350\t        }\n351\t    }\n352\t\n353\t    // Trailing statement without a final semicolon.\n354\t    if start < len {\n355\t        let stmt = &sql[start..len];\n356\t        if !stmt.trim().is_empty() {\n357\t            stmts.push(stmt.trim());\n358\t        }\n359\t    }\n360\t\n361\t    stmts\n362\t}\n363\t\n364\t/// Execute multiple SQL statements separated by semicolons.\n365\t///\n366\t/// fsqlite does not support `execute_batch`, so we split the SQL script\n367\t/// into individual statements (respecting string literals and comments)\n368\t/// and execute each one individually.\n369\tpub(crate) fn execute_batch(conn: &Connection, sql: &str) -> Result<()> {\n370\t    for stmt in split_sql_statements(sql) {\n371\t        conn.execute(stmt)?;\n372\t    }\n373\t    Ok(())\n374\t}\n375\t\n376\t/// Apply the schema to the database.\n377\t///\n378\t/// This splits the DDL script into individual statements and executes them.\n379\t/// It is idempotent because all statements use `IF NOT EXISTS`.\n380\t///\n381\t/// # Errors\n382\t///\n383\t/// Returns an error if the SQL execution fails or pragmas cannot be set.\n384\tpub fn apply_schema(conn: &Connection) -> Result<()> {\n385\t    // Run pre-schema migrations first to fix any incompatible old tables\n386\t    // This must run BEFORE execute_batch because the batch includes CREATE INDEX\n387\t    // statements that will fail if old tables have missing columns\n388\t    run_pre_schema_migrations(conn)?;\n389\t\n390\t    execute_batch(conn, SCHEMA_SQL)?;\n391\t\n392\t    // Run migrations for existing databases\n393\t    run_migrations(conn)?;\n394\t\n395\t    apply_runtime_pragmas(conn)?;\n396\t\n397\t    // Mark schema as applied so future opens can skip DDL/migration work.\n398\t    conn.execute(&format!(\"PRAGMA user_version = {CURRENT_SCHEMA_VERSION}\"))?;\n399\t\n400\t    Ok(())\n401\t}\n402\t\n403\tpub(crate) fn apply_runtime_compatible_schema(conn: &Connection) -> Result<()> {\n404\t    // The table layouts are already safe to operate on, so we can skip the\n405\t    // heavier pre-schema rebuilds and just restore any missing canonical DDL.\n406\t    execute_batch(conn, SCHEMA_SQL)?;\n407\t    run_migrations(conn)?;\n408\t    apply_runtime_pragmas(conn)?;\n409\t    conn.execute(&format!(\"PRAGMA user_version = {CURRENT_SCHEMA_VERSION}\"))?;\n410\t    Ok(())\n411\t}\n412\t\n413\tpub(crate) fn apply_runtime_pragmas(conn: &Connection) -> Result<()> {\n414\t    // New databases should opt into WAL, but steady-state opens should not\n415\t    // reassert the current mode and turn a read path into a write-like one.\n416\t    let journal_mode = conn\n417\t        .query_row(\"PRAGMA journal_mode\")\n418\t        .ok()\n419\t        .and_then(|row| row.get(0).and_then(SqliteValue::as_text).map(str::to_owned))\n420\t        .unwrap_or_default();\n421\t    if !journal_mode.eq_ignore_ascii_case(\"wal\") {\n422\t        conn.execute(\"PRAGMA journal_mode = WAL\")?;\n423\t    }\n424\t\n425\t    // Enable foreign keys\n426\t    conn.execute(\"PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON\")?;\n427\t\n428\t    // Performance PRAGMAs (safe with WAL mode)\n429\t    // NORMAL synchronous is safe with WAL: committed data survives OS crash\n430\t    conn.execute(\"PRAGMA synchronous = NORMAL\")?;\n431\t    // Use memory for temp tables/indexes instead of disk\n432\t    conn.execute(\"PRAGMA temp_store = MEMORY\")?;\n433\t    // 8MB page cache (default is ~2MB), improves read-heavy workloads\n434\t    conn.execute(\"PRAGMA cache_size = -8000\")?;\n435\t\n436\t    Ok(())\n437\t}\n438\t\n439\tfn table_exists(conn: &Connection, table: &str) -> bool {\n440\t    let escaped_table = table.replace('\\'', \"''\");\n441\t    let sql = format!(\"SELECT 1 FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='{escaped_table}'\");\n442\t    conn.query(&sql).is_ok_and(|rows| !rows.is_empty())\n443\t}\n444\t\n445\tfn index_exists(conn: &Connection, index: &str) -> bool {\n446\t    let escaped_index = index.replace('\\'', \"''\");\n447\t    let sql = format!(\"SELECT 1 FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='index' AND name='{escaped_index}'\");\n448\t    conn.query(&sql).is_ok_and(|rows| !rows.is_empty())\n449\t}\n450\t\n451\tfn column_exists(conn: &Connection, table: &str, column: &str) -> bool {\n452\t    let sql = format!(\"PRAGMA table_info('{table}')\");\n453\t    conn.query(&sql).is_ok_and(|rows| {\n454\t        rows.iter()\n455\t            .any(|row| row.get(1).and_then(SqliteValue::as_text) == Some(column))\n456\t    })\n457\t}\n458\t\n459\tconst ISSUE_COLUMNS: &[(&str, &str)] = &[\n460\t    (\"content_hash\", \"TEXT\"),\n461\t    (\"description\", \"TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT ''\"),\n462\t    (\"design\", \"TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT ''\"),\n463\t    (\"acceptance_criteria\", \"TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT ''\"),\n464\t    (\"notes\", \"TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT ''\"),\n465\t    (\"status\", \"TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'open'\"),\n466\t    (\n467\t        \"priority\",\n468\t        \"INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 2 CHECK(priority >= 0 AND priority <= 4)\",\n469\t    ),\n470\t    (\"issue_type\", \"TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'task'\"),\n471\t    (\"assignee\", \"TEXT\"),\n472\t    (\"owner\", \"TEXT DEFAULT ''\"),\n473\t    (\"estimated_minutes\", \"INTEGER\"),\n474\t    (\"created_at\", \"DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP\"),\n475\t    (\"created_by\", \"TEXT DEFAULT ''\"),\n476\t    (\"updated_at\", \"DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP\"),\n477\t    (\"closed_at\", \"DATETIME\"),\n478\t    (\"close_reason\", \"TEXT DEFAULT ''\"),\n479\t    (\"closed_by_session\", \"TEXT DEFAULT ''\"),\n480\t    (\"due_at\", \"DATETIME\"),\n481\t    (\"defer_until\", \"DATETIME\"),\n482\t    (\"external_ref\", \"TEXT\"),\n483\t    (\"source_system\", \"TEXT DEFAULT ''\"),\n484\t    (\"source_repo\", \"TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '.'\"),\n485\t    (\"deleted_at\", \"DATETIME\"),\n486\t    (\"deleted_by\", \"TEXT DEFAULT ''\"),\n487\t    (\"delete_reason\", \"TEXT DEFAULT ''\"),\n488\t    (\"original_type\", \"TEXT DEFAULT ''\"),\n489\t    (\"compaction_level\", \"INTEGER DEFAULT 0\"),\n490\t    (\"compacted_at\", \"DATETIME\"),\n491\t    (\"compacted_at_commit\", \"TEXT\"),\n492\t    (\"original_size\", \"INTEGER\"),\n493\t    (\"sender\", \"TEXT DEFAULT ''\"),\n494\t    (\"ephemeral\", \"INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0\"),\n495\t    (\"pinned\", \"INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0\"),\n496\t    (\"is_template\", \"INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0\"),\n497\t];\n498\t\n499\tconst DEPENDENCY_COLUMNS: &[(&str, &str)] = &[\n500\t    (\"type\", \"TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'blocks'\"),\n501\t    (\"created_at\", \"DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP\"),\n502\t    (\"created_by\", \"TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT ''\"),\n503\t    (\"metadata\", \"TEXT DEFAULT '{}'\"),\n504\t    (\"thread_id\", \"TEXT DEFAULT ''\"),\n505\t];\n506\t\n507\tconst COMMENT_COLUMNS: &[(&str, &str)] = &[\n508\t    (\"author\", \"TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT ''\"),\n509\t    (\"text\", \"TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT ''\"),\n510\t    (\"created_at\", \"DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP\"),\n511\t];\n512\t\n513\tconst EVENT_COLUMNS: &[(&str, &str)] = &[\n514\t    (\"event_type\", \"TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT ''\"),\n515\t    (\"actor\", \"TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT ''\"),\n516\t    (\"old_value\", \"TEXT\"),\n517\t    (\"new_value\", \"TEXT\"),\n518\t    (\"comment\", \"TEXT\"),\n519\t    (\"created_at\", \"DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP\"),\n520\t];\n521\t\n522\tfn ensure_columns(conn: &Connection, table: &str, columns: &[(&str, &str)]) -> Result<()> {\n523\t    if !table_exists(conn, table) {\n524\t        return Ok(());\n525\t    }\n526\t\n527\t    for (name, definition) in columns {\n528\t        if !column_exists(conn, table, name) {\n529\t            let sql = format!(\"ALTER TABLE {table} ADD COLUMN {name} {definition}\");\n530\t            conn.execute(&sql)?;\n531\t        }\n532\t    }\n533\t\n534\t    Ok(())\n535\t}\n536\t\n537\tfn table_has_columns(conn: &Connection, table: &str, required_columns: &[&str]) -> bool {\n538\t    table_exists(conn, table)\n539\t        && required_columns\n540\t            .iter()\n541\t            .all(|column| column_exists(conn, table, column))\n542\t}\n543\t\n544\tfn current_schema_version_declared(conn: &Connection) -> bool {\n545\t    conn.query_row(\"PRAGMA user_version\")\n546\t        .ok()\n547\t        .and_then(|row| row.get(0).and_then(SqliteValue::as_integer))\n548\t        .is_some_and(|version| version >= i64::from(CURRENT_SCHEMA_VERSION))\n549\t}\n550\t\n551\tfn core_runtime_tables_exist(conn: &Connection) -> bool {\n552\t    [\n553\t        \"issues\",\n554\t        \"dependencies\",\n555\t        \"labels\",\n556\t        \"comments\",\n557\t        \"events\",\n558\t        \"config\",\n559\t        \"metadata\",\n560\t        \"dirty_issues\",\n561\t        \"export_hashes\",\n562\t        \"blocked_issues_cache\",\n563\t        \"child_counters\",\n564\t        \"recovery_sessions\",\n565\t        \"anomaly_audit\",\n566\t    ]\n567\t    .iter()\n568\t    .all(|table| table_exists(conn, table))\n569\t}\n570\t\n571\t/// Expected column order for the issues table (id + ISSUE_COLUMNS names).\n572\t/// Used to detect when ALTER TABLE has appended columns in the wrong position,\n573\t/// which causes fsqlite to fail with \"no such column\" errors on older databases.\n574\tconst EXPECTED_ISSUE_COLUMN_ORDER: &[&str] = &[\n575\t    \"id\",\n576\t    \"content_hash\",\n577\t    \"title\",\n578\t    \"description\",\n579\t    \"design\",\n580\t    \"acceptance_criteria\",\n581\t    \"notes\",\n582\t    \"status\",\n583\t    \"priority\",\n584\t    \"issue_type\",\n585\t    \"assignee\",\n586\t    \"owner\",\n587\t    \"estimated_minutes\",\n588\t    \"created_at\",\n589\t    \"created_by\",\n590\t    \"updated_at\",\n591\t    \"closed_at\",\n592\t    \"close_reason\",\n593\t    \"closed_by_session\",\n594\t    \"due_at\",\n595\t    \"defer_until\",\n596\t    \"external_ref\",\n597\t    \"source_system\",\n598\t    \"source_repo\",\n599\t    \"deleted_at\",\n600\t    \"deleted_by\",\n601\t    \"delete_reason\",\n602\t    \"original_type\",\n603\t    \"compaction_level\",\n604\t    \"compacted_at\",\n605\t    \"compacted_at_commit\",\n606\t    \"original_size\",\n607\t    \"sender\",\n608\t    \"ephemeral\",\n609\t    \"pinned\",\n610\t    \"is_template\",\n611\t];\n612\t\n613\t/// Check whether the issues table has columns in the expected order.\n614\t/// Returns `true` if the column order matches, `false` if it differs or the\n615\t/// table doesn't exist.\n616\tfn issues_column_order_matches(conn: &Connection) -> bool {\n617\t    if !table_exists(conn, \"issues\") {\n618\t        return true; // Will be created fresh by SCHEMA_SQL\n619\t    }\n620\t\n621\t    let Ok(rows) = conn.query(\"PRAGMA table_info(issues)\") else {\n622\t        return false;\n623\t    };\n624\t\n625\t    let actual_columns: Vec<String> = rows\n626\t        .iter()\n627\t        .filter_map(|row| row.get(1).and_then(SqliteValue::as_text).map(String::from))\n628\t        .collect();\n629\t\n630\t    if actual_columns.len() != EXPECTED_ISSUE_COLUMN_ORDER.len() {\n631\t        return false;\n632\t    }\n633\t\n634\t    actual_columns\n635\t        .iter()\n636\t        .zip(EXPECTED_ISSUE_COLUMN_ORDER.iter())\n637\t        .all(|(actual, expected)| actual == expected)\n638\t}\n639\t\n640\tfn issues_filter_columns_require_v3_rebuild(conn: &Connection) -> bool {\n641\t    let Ok(rows) = conn.query(\"PRAGMA table_info('issues')\") else {\n642\t        return true;\n643\t    };\n644\t\n645\t    for column in [\"ephemeral\", \"pinned\", \"is_template\"] {\n646\t        let Some(row) = rows\n647\t            .iter()\n648\t            .find(|row| row.get(1).and_then(SqliteValue::as_text) == Some(column))\n649\t        else {\n650\t            return true;\n651\t        };\n652\t\n653\t        let not_null = row.get(3).and_then(SqliteValue::as_integer).unwrap_or(0);\n654\t        if not_null == 0 {\n655\t            return true;\n656\t        }\n657\t    }\n658\t\n659\t    false\n660\t}\n661\t\n662\t/// Rebuild the issues table so columns match the canonical SCHEMA_SQL order.\n663\t///\n664\t/// This fixes databases where ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN appended columns in a\n665\t/// different position than the CREATE TABLE definition, causing fsqlite's\n666\t/// column-name resolver to fail with \"no such column\" errors.\n667\t///\n668\t/// Uses the standard SQLite migration pattern:\n669\t///   1. Create new table with correct schema\n670\t///   2. Copy data from old table\n671\t///   3. Drop old table\n672\t///   4. Rename new table\n673\tfn rebuild_issues_table(conn: &Connection) -> Result<()> {\n674\t    let existing_rows = conn.query(\"PRAGMA table_info('issues')\")?;\n675\t    let existing_columns: Vec<String> = existing_rows\n676\t        .iter()\n677\t        .filter_map(|row| row.get(1).and_then(SqliteValue::as_text).map(String::from))\n678\t        .collect();\n679\t\n680\t    if existing_columns.is_empty() {\n681\t        return Ok(()); // Table is empty or doesn't exist\n682\t    }\n683\t\n684\t    // Disable foreign keys during the rebuild because we'll be dropping\n685\t    // and recreating the issues table which is referenced by other tables.\n686\t    // This property is connection-scoped.\n687\t    conn.execute(\"PRAGMA foreign_keys = OFF\")?;\n688\t\n689\t    // Wrap the entire rebuild in a transaction so a crash between DROP TABLE\n690\t    // and RENAME cannot lose data.\n691\t    conn.execute(\"BEGIN EXCLUSIVE\")?;\n692\t\n693\t    if let Err(e) = rebuild_issues_table_inner(conn, &existing_columns) {\n694\t        let _ = conn.execute(\"ROLLBACK\");\n695\t        let _ = conn.execute(\"PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON\");\n696\t        return Err(e);\n697\t    }\n698\t\n699\t    conn.execute(\"COMMIT\")?;\n700\t\n701\t    // Restore foreign key enforcement\n702\t    conn.execute(\"PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON\")?;\n703\t\n704\t    Ok(())\n705\t}\n706\t\n707\t/// Inner helper for [`rebuild_issues_table`] that performs the actual work\n708\t/// inside an already-open transaction.\n709\tfn rebuild_issues_table_inner(conn: &Connection, existing_columns: &[String]) -> Result<()> {\n710\t    // Drop all indexes on the issues table first (they'll be recreated by SCHEMA_SQL)\n711\t    let index_rows =\n712\t        conn.query(\"SELECT name FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='index' AND tbl_name='issues' AND sql IS NOT NULL\")?;\n713\t    for row in &index_rows {\n714\t        if let Some(name) = row.get(0).and_then(SqliteValue::as_text) {\n715\t            conn.execute(&format!(\"DROP INDEX IF EXISTS \\\"{name}\\\"\"))?;\n716\t        }\n717\t    }\n718\t\n719\t    // Drop tables that have foreign keys referencing issues (they'll be recreated)\n720\t    // We need to save and restore their data too.\n721\t    // For simplicity, we only rebuild the issues table and let SCHEMA_SQL\n722\t    // recreate indexes. Foreign key tables (dependencies, labels, etc.) keep\n723\t    // their data since we use the same primary key.\n724\t\n725\t    // Create the new table with canonical column order\n726\t    // Use a temporary name to avoid conflicts\n727\t    conn.execute(\"DROP TABLE IF EXISTS issues_rebuild_tmp\")?;\n728\t\n729\t    // Build CREATE TABLE for the new table with only columns that exist in the old table\n730\t    // plus any missing columns with defaults\n731\t    // Build the canonical column list: id, content_hash, title, then the\n732\t    // rest of ISSUE_COLUMNS (skipping content_hash which is already placed).\n733\t    // This order must match EXPECTED_ISSUE_COLUMN_ORDER and SCHEMA_SQL.\n734\t    let all_expected: Vec<(&str, &str)> = std::iter::once((\"id\", \"TEXT PRIMARY KEY\"))\n735\t        .chain(std::iter::once((\"content_hash\", \"TEXT\")))\n736\t        .chain(std::iter::once((\n737\t            \"title\",\n738\t            \"TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(length(title) <= 500)\",\n739\t        )))\n740\t        .chain(\n741\t            ISSUE_COLUMNS\n742\t                .iter()\n743\t                .copied()\n744\t                .filter(|(name, _)| *name != \"content_hash\"),\n745\t        )\n746\t        .collect();\n747\t\n748\t    let mut create_cols = Vec::new();\n749\t    for (col_name, col_def) in &all_expected {\n750\t        create_cols.push(format!(\"{col_name} {col_def}\"));\n751\t    }\n752\t    create_cols.push(ISSUES_CLOSED_AT_CHECK.to_string());\n753\t\n754\t    let create_sql = format!(\n755\t        \"CREATE TABLE issues_rebuild_tmp ({})\",\n756\t        create_cols.join(\", \")\n757\t    );\n758\t    conn.execute(&create_sql)?;\n759\t\n760\t    // Copy only columns that exist in the source table so SQLite can apply\n761\t    // declared defaults for newer columns that are absent in legacy schemas.\n762\t    let mut projected_columns = Vec::new();\n763\t    for (col_name, _) in &all_expected {\n764\t        if existing_columns.iter().any(|c| c == col_name) {\n765\t            projected_columns.push((*col_name).to_string());\n766\t        }\n767\t    }\n768\t\n769\t    if projected_columns.is_empty() {\n770\t        return Err(BeadsError::Config(\n771\t            \"Cannot rebuild legacy issues table: no canonical issue columns were found\".to_string(),\n772\t        ));\n773\t    }\n774\t\n775\t    let copy_sql = format!(\n776\t        \"INSERT INTO issues_rebuild_tmp ({}) SELECT {} FROM issues\",\n777\t        projected_columns.join(\", \"),\n778\t        projected_columns.join(\", \")\n779\t    );\n780\t    conn.execute(&copy_sql)?;\n781\t\n782\t    // Swap tables\n783\t    conn.execute(\"DROP TABLE issues\")?;\n784\t    conn.execute(\"ALTER TABLE issues_rebuild_tmp RENAME TO issues\")?;\n785\t\n786\t    Ok(())\n787\t}\n788\t\n789\tfn kv_table_uses_primary_key(conn: &Connection, table: &str) -> bool {\n790\t    if !table_exists(conn, table) {\n791\t        return false;\n792\t    }\n793\t\n794\t    let escaped_table = table.replace('\\'', \"''\");\n795\t    let sql =\n796\t        format!(\"SELECT sql FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='{escaped_table}'\");\n797\t    conn.query_row(&sql)\n798\t        .ok()\n799\t        .and_then(|row| row.get(0).and_then(SqliteValue::as_text).map(str::to_owned))\n800\t        .is_some_and(|ddl| ddl.to_ascii_uppercase().contains(\"PRIMARY KEY\"))\n801\t}\n802\t\n803\tfn kv_table_needs_canonical_rebuild(conn: &Connection, table: &str, expected_index: &str) -> bool {\n804\t    table_exists(conn, table)\n805\t        && (!index_exists(conn, expected_index) || kv_table_uses_primary_key(conn, table))\n806\t}\n807\t\n808\tfn rebuild_kv_table_without_unique(conn: &Connection, table: &str) -> Result<()> {\n809\t    let tmp_table = format!(\"{table}_rebuild_tmp\");\n810\t\n811\t    conn.execute(\"BEGIN EXCLUSIVE\")?;\n812\t\n813\t    let result = (|| -> Result<()> {\n814\t        conn.execute(&format!(\"DROP TABLE IF EXISTS {tmp_table}\"))?;\n815\t        conn.execute(&format!(\n816\t            \"CREATE TABLE {tmp_table} (\n817\t                key TEXT NOT NULL,\n818\t                value TEXT NOT NULL\n819\t            )\"\n820\t        ))?;\n821\t\n822\t        conn.execute(&format!(\n823\t            \"INSERT INTO {tmp_table} (key, value)\n824\t             SELECT key, value\n825\t             FROM {table}\"\n826\t        ))?;\n827\t\n828\t        conn.execute(&format!(\"DROP TABLE {table}\"))?;\n829\t        conn.execute(&format!(\"ALTER TABLE {tmp_table} RENAME TO {table}\"))?;\n830\t        Ok(())\n831\t    })();\n832\t\n833\t    if let Err(err) = result {\n834\t        let _ = conn.execute(\"ROLLBACK\");\n835\t        return Err(err);\n836\t    }\n837\t\n838\t    conn.execute(\"COMMIT\")?;\n839\t    Ok(())\n840\t}\n841\t\n842\t/// Run pre-schema migrations to fix incompatible old tables.\n843\t///\n844\t/// This must run BEFORE `execute_batch(SCHEMA_SQL)` because the schema includes\n845\t/// CREATE INDEX statements that will fail if old tables have missing columns.\n846\tfn run_pre_schema_migrations(conn: &Connection) -> Result<()> {\n847\t    // Legacy schemas used PRIMARY KEY on config/metadata key columns.\n848\t    // Rebuild to plain key-value tables so standard sqlite integrity checks\n849\t    // are not tripped by unsupported unique-index maintenance behavior.\n850\t    if kv_table_needs_canonical_rebuild(conn, \"config\", \"idx_config_key\") {\n851\t        rebuild_kv_table_without_unique(conn, \"config\")?;\n852\t    }\n853\t    if kv_table_needs_canonical_rebuild(conn, \"metadata\", \"idx_metadata_key\") {\n854\t        rebuild_kv_table_without_unique(conn, \"metadata\")?;\n855\t    }\n856\t\n857\t    // Drop blocked_issues_cache if it exists but lacks required columns.\n858\t    // The main schema will recreate it with the correct structure.\n859\t    if table_exists(conn, \"blocked_issues_cache\") {\n860\t        let has_blocked_at = column_exists(conn, \"blocked_issues_cache\", \"blocked_at\");\n861\t        let has_blocked_by = column_exists(conn, \"blocked_issues_cache\", \"blocked_by\");\n862\t        let has_issue_id = column_exists(conn, \"blocked_issues_cache\", \"issue_id\");\n863\t\n864\t        if !has_blocked_at || !has_blocked_by || !has_issue_id {\n865\t            conn.execute(\"DROP TABLE IF EXISTS blocked_issues_cache\")?;\n866\t        }\n867\t    }\n868\t\n869\t    // Rebuild the issues table if columns are out of order or missing.\n870\t    // This fixes fsqlite \"no such column\" errors on databases created with\n871\t    // older br versions where ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN appended columns in\n872\t    // a different position than the canonical CREATE TABLE definition.\n873\t    if table_exists(conn, \"issues\") && !issues_column_order_matches(conn) {\n874\t        rebuild_issues_table(conn)?;\n875\t    }\n876\t\n877\t    // Ensure legacy tables have all columns needed for schema indexes and queries.\n878\t    ensure_columns(conn, \"issues\", ISSUE_COLUMNS)?;\n879\t    ensure_columns(conn, \"dependencies\", DEPENDENCY_COLUMNS)?;\n880\t    ensure_columns(conn, \"comments\", COMMENT_COLUMNS)?;\n881\t    ensure_columns(conn, \"events\", EVENT_COLUMNS)?;\n882\t\n883\t    // Intentionally do not rebuild idx_issues_ready here.\n884\t    //\n885\t    // Older databases may have a stale partial-index predicate, but that is a\n886\t    // performance issue rather than a correctness issue. On large file-backed\n887\t    // databases, exercising DROP INDEX through frankensqlite currently trips an\n888\t    // out-of-memory failure. Additionally, frankensqlite's in-memory schema\n889\t    // representation does not reliably preserve partial-index predicates, so br\n890\t    // cannot distinguish a stale ready index from a current one at open time.\n891\t\n892\t    Ok(())\n893\t}\n894\t\n895\tpub(crate) fn runtime_schema_compatible(conn: &Connection) -> bool {\n896\t    if current_schema_version_declared(conn) && core_runtime_tables_exist(conn) {\n897\t        return true;\n898\t    }\n899\t\n900\t    let issues_ok = issues_column_order_matches(conn);\n901\t    let dependencies_ok = table_has_columns(conn, \"dependencies\", &[\"issue_id\", \"depends_on_id\"])\n902\t        && DEPENDENCY_COLUMNS\n903\t            .iter()\n904\t            .all(|(name, _)| column_exists(conn, \"dependencies\", name));\n905\t    let labels_ok = table_has_columns(conn, \"labels\", &[\"issue_id\", \"label\"]);\n906\t    let comments_ok = table_has_columns(conn, \"comments\", &[\"id\", \"issue_id\"])\n907\t        && COMMENT_COLUMNS\n908\t            .iter()\n909\t            .all(|(name, _)| column_exists(conn, \"comments\", name));\n910\t    let events_ok = table_has_columns(conn, \"events\", &[\"id\", \"issue_id\"])\n911\t        && EVENT_COLUMNS\n912\t            .iter()\n913\t            .all(|(name, _)| column_exists(conn, \"events\", name));\n914\t    let config_ok = table_has_columns(conn, \"config\", &[\"key\", \"value\"])\n915\t        && index_exists(conn, \"idx_config_key\")\n916\t        && !kv_table_uses_primary_key(conn, \"config\");\n917\t    let metadata_ok = table_has_columns(conn, \"metadata\", &[\"key\", \"value\"])\n918\t        && index_exists(conn, \"idx_metadata_key\")\n919\t        && !kv_table_uses_primary_key(conn, \"metadata\");\n920\t    let dirty_issues_ok = table_has_columns(conn, \"dirty_issues\", &[\"issue_id\", \"marked_at\"]);\n921\t    let export_hashes_ok = table_has_columns(\n922\t        conn,\n923\t        \"export_hashes\",\n924\t        &[\"issue_id\", \"content_hash\", \"exported_at\"],\n925\t    );\n926\t    let blocked_cache_ok = table_has_columns(\n927\t        conn,\n928\t        \"blocked_issues_cache\",\n929\t        &[\"issue_id\", \"blocked_by\", \"blocked_at\"],\n930\t    );\n931\t    let child_counters_ok = table_has_columns(conn, \"child_counters\", &[\"parent_id\", \"last_child\"]);\n932\t\n933\t    // Audit tables are optional for compatibility - if they exist, check their schema\n934\t    let recovery_sessions_ok = !table_exists(conn, \"recovery_sessions\")\n935\t        || table_has_columns(\n936\t            conn,\n937\t            \"recovery_sessions\",\n938\t            &[\"correlation_id\", \"started_at\", \"session_type\"],\n939\t        );\n940\t    let anomaly_audit_ok = !table_exists(conn, \"anomaly_audit\")\n941\t        || table_has_columns(\n942\t            conn,\n943\t            \"anomaly_audit\",\n944\t            &[\"correlation_id\", \"timestamp\", \"event_type\"],\n945\t        );\n946\t\n947\t    let compatible = issues_ok\n948\t        && dependencies_ok\n949\t        && labels_ok\n950\t        && comments_ok\n951\t        && events_ok\n952\t        && config_ok\n953\t        && metadata_ok\n954\t        && dirty_issues_ok\n955\t        && export_hashes_ok\n956\t        && blocked_cache_ok\n957\t        && child_counters_ok\n958\t        && recovery_sessions_ok\n959\t        && anomaly_audit_ok;\n960\t\n961\t    if !compatible {\n962\t        tracing::debug!(\n963\t            issues_ok,\n964\t            dependencies_ok,\n965\t            labels_ok,\n966\t            comments_ok,\n967\t            events_ok,\n968\t            config_ok,\n969\t            metadata_ok,\n970\t            dirty_issues_ok,\n971\t            export_hashes_ok,\n972\t            blocked_cache_ok,\n973\t            child_counters_ok,\n974\t            recovery_sessions_ok,\n975\t            anomaly_audit_ok,\n976\t            \"runtime schema compatibility check failed\"\n977\t        );\n978\t    }\n979\t\n980\t    compatible\n981\t}\n982\t\n983\t/// Run schema migrations for existing databases.\n984\t///\n985\t/// This handles upgrades for tables that may have been created with older schemas.\n986\t#[allow(clippy::too_many_lines)]\n987\tfn run_migrations(conn: &Connection) -> Result<()> {\n988\t    // Migration: Ensure blocked_issues_cache has correct schema (blocked_by, blocked_at)\n989\t    // Check for old column name (blocked_by_json) or missing columns\n990\t    let has_blocked_by = column_exists(conn, \"blocked_issues_cache\", \"blocked_by\");\n991\t    let has_blocked_at = column_exists(conn, \"blocked_issues_cache\", \"blocked_at\");\n992\t    let has_issue_id = column_exists(conn, \"blocked_issues_cache\", \"issue_id\");\n993\t\n994\t    if !has_blocked_by || !has_blocked_at || !has_issue_id {\n995\t        // Table needs update - drop and recreate (it's a cache, data is regenerated)\n996\t        // Wrap in transaction so concurrent opens don't see a partially migrated state\n997\t        conn.execute(\"BEGIN IMMEDIATE\")?;\n998\t        let result = (|| -> Result<()> {\n999\t            conn.execute(\"DROP TABLE IF EXISTS blocked_issues_cache\")?;\n1000\t            conn.execute(\n1001\t                \"CREATE TABLE blocked_issues_cache (\n1002\t                    issue_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n1003\t                    blocked_by TEXT NOT NULL,\n1004\t                    blocked_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,\n1005\t                    FOREIGN KEY (issue_id) REFERENCES issues(id) ON DELETE CASCADE\n1006\t                )\",\n1007\t            )?;\n1008\t            conn.execute(\n1009\t                \"CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_blocked_cache_blocked_at ON blocked_issues_cache(blocked_at)\",\n1010\t            )?;\n1011\t            Ok(())\n1012\t        })();\n1013\t\n1014\t        if let Err(e) = result {\n1015\t            let _ = conn.execute(\"ROLLBACK\");\n1016\t            return Err(e);\n1017\t        }\n1018\t        conn.execute(\"COMMIT\")?;\n1019\t    }\n1020\t\n1021\t    // Migration: ensure compaction_level is never NULL (bd compatibility)\n1022\t    let has_compaction_level = column_exists(conn, \"issues\", \"compaction_level\");\n1023\t\n1024\t    if has_compaction_level {\n1025\t        conn.execute(\"UPDATE issues SET compaction_level = 0 WHERE compaction_level IS NULL\")?;\n1026\t    }\n1027\t\n1028\t    // Migration: Ensure filter columns are NOT NULL (v3)\n1029\t    let user_version = conn\n1030\t        .query_row(\"PRAGMA user_version\")?\n1031\t        .get(0)\n1032\t        .and_then(SqliteValue::as_integer)\n1033\t        .unwrap_or(0);\n1034\t\n1035\t    if user_version < 3\n1036\t        && table_exists(conn, \"issues\")\n1037\t        && issues_filter_columns_require_v3_rebuild(conn)\n1038\t    {\n1039\t        tracing::info!(\"Migrating database to schema version 3 (NOT NULL filter columns)\");\n1040\t        // 1. Backfill NULL values\n1041\t        conn.execute(\"UPDATE issues SET ephemeral = 0 WHERE ephemeral IS NULL\")?;\n1042\t        conn.execute(\"UPDATE issues SET pinned = 0 WHERE pinned IS NULL\")?;\n1043\t        conn.execute(\"UPDATE issues SET is_template = 0 WHERE is_template IS NULL\")?;\n1044\t\n1045\t        // 2. Rebuild the table to apply NOT NULL constraints\n1046\t        rebuild_issues_table(conn)?;\n1047\t\n1048\t        // 3. Recreate the optimized ready index\n1049\t        conn.execute(\"DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_issues_ready\")?;\n1050\t        conn.execute(\n1051\t            \"CREATE INDEX idx_issues_ready\n1052\t             ON issues(status, priority, created_at)\n1053\t             WHERE status = 'open'\n1054\t             AND ephemeral = 0\n1055\t             AND pinned = 0\n1056\t             AND is_template = 0\",\n1057\t        )?;\n1058\t    }\n1059\t\n1060\t    if user_version < 4 && table_exists(conn, \"issues\") {\n1061\t        tracing::info!(\"Migrating database to schema version 4 (ready excludes in_progress)\");\n1062\t        conn.execute(\"DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_issues_ready\")?;\n1063\t        conn.execute(\n1064\t            \"CREATE INDEX idx_issues_ready\n1065\t             ON issues(status, priority, created_at)\n1066\t             WHERE status = 'open'\n1067\t             AND ephemeral = 0\n1068\t             AND pinned = 0\n1069\t             AND is_template = 0\",\n1070\t        )?;\n1071\t    }\n1072\t\n1073\t    // Note: source_repo and is_template column backfills are handled in\n1074\t    // run_pre_schema_migrations() via ensure_columns(). Repeating ALTER TABLE\n1075\t    // here can create duplicate column definitions on some engines.\n1076\t\n1077\t    // Migration: Add missing indexes for bd parity\n1078\t    // These use IF NOT EXISTS so they're safe to run multiple times\n1079\t    execute_batch(\n1080\t        conn,\n1081\t        r\"\n1082\t        -- Export/sync patterns\n1083\t        CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_issues_content_hash ON issues(content_hash);\n1084\t        CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_issues_external_ref ON issues(external_ref) WHERE external_ref IS NOT NULL;\n1085\t        CREATE UNIQUE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_issues_external_ref_unique ON issues(external_ref) WHERE external_ref IS NOT NULL;\n1086\t\n1087\t        -- Special states\n1088\t        CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_issues_ephemeral ON issues(ephemeral) WHERE ephemeral = 1;\n1089\t        CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_issues_pinned ON issues(pinned) WHERE pinned = 1;\n1090\t        CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_issues_tombstone ON issues(status) WHERE status = 'tombstone';\n1091\t\n1092\t        -- Time-based\n1093\t        CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_issues_due_at ON issues(due_at) WHERE due_at IS NOT NULL;\n1094\t        CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_issues_defer_until ON issues(defer_until) WHERE defer_until IS NOT NULL;\n1095\t\n1096\t        -- Ready work composite index (most important for performance)\n1097\t        CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_issues_ready\n1098\t            ON issues(status, priority, created_at)\n1099\t            WHERE status = 'open'\n1100\t            AND ephemeral = 0\n1101\t            AND pinned = 0\n1102\t            AND is_template = 0;\n1103\t\n1104\t        -- Common active list path: non-terminal issues sorted by priority/created_at\n1105\t        CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_issues_list_active_order\n1106\t            ON issues(priority, created_at DESC)\n1107\t            WHERE status NOT IN ('closed', 'tombstone')\n1108\t            AND (is_template = 0 OR is_template IS NULL);\n1109\t\n1110\t    \",\n1111\t    )?;\n1112\t\n1113\t    // Drop legacy index names (safe if absent)\n1114\t    execute_batch(\n1115\t        conn,\n1116\t        r\"\n1117\t        DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_dependencies_issue_id;\n1118\t        DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_dependencies_depends_on_id;\n1119\t        DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_dependencies_composite;\n1120\t        DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_labels_issue_id;\n1121\t    \",\n1122\t    )?;\n1123\t\n1124\t    if table_exists(conn, \"dependencies\") {\n1125\t        execute_batch(\n1126\t            conn,\n1127\t            r\"\n1128\t            CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_dependencies_issue ON dependencies(issue_id);\n1129\t            CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_dependencies_depends_on ON dependencies(depends_on_id);\n1130\t            CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_dependencies_type ON dependencies(type);\n1131\t            CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_dependencies_depends_on_type ON dependencies(depends_on_id, type);\n1132\t            CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_dependencies_blocking\n1133\t                ON dependencies(depends_on_id, issue_id)\n1134\t                WHERE (type = 'blocks' OR type = 'parent-child' OR type = 'conditional-blocks' OR type = 'waits-for');\n1135\t        \",\n1136\t        )?;\n1137\t\n1138\t        if column_exists(conn, \"dependencies\", \"thread_id\") {\n1139\t            conn.execute(\n1140\t                \"CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_dependencies_thread ON dependencies(thread_id) WHERE thread_id != ''\",\n1141\t            )?;\n1142\t        }\n1143\t    }\n1144\t\n1145\t    if table_exists(conn, \"labels\") {\n1146\t        execute_batch(\n1147\t            conn,\n1148\t            r\"\n1149\t            CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_labels_label ON labels(label);\n1150\t            CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_labels_issue ON labels(issue_id);\n1151\t        \",\n1152\t        )?;\n1153\t    }\n1154\t\n1155\t    if table_exists(conn, \"comments\") {\n1156\t        conn.execute(\"CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_comments_issue ON comments(issue_id)\")?;\n1157\t    }\n1158\t\n1159\t    if table_exists(conn, \"events\") {\n1160\t        execute_batch(\n1161\t            conn,\n1162\t            r\"\n1163\t            CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_events_issue ON events(issue_id);\n1164\t            CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_events_type ON events(event_type);\n1165\t            CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_events_actor ON events(actor) WHERE actor != '';\n1166\t        \",\n1167\t        )?;\n1168\t    }\n1169\t\n1170\t    Ok(())\n1171\t}\n1172\t\n1173\t#[cfg(test)]\n1174\tmod tests {\n1175\t    use super::*;\n1176\t    use crate::error::BeadsError;\n1177\t    use crate::storage::db::Connection;\n1178\t    use std::collections::HashSet;\n1179\t    use tempfile::TempDir;\n1180\t\n1181\t    #[test]\n1182\t    fn test_apply_schema() {\n1183\t        let conn = Connection::open(\":memory:\").unwrap();\n1184\t        apply_schema(&conn).expect(\"Failed to apply schema\");\n1185\t\n1186\t        // Verify a few tables exist\n1187\t        let tables: Vec<String> = conn\n1188\t            .query(\"SELECT name FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table'\")\n1189\t            .unwrap()\n1190\t            .iter()\n1191\t            .filter_map(|row| row.get(0).and_then(|v| v.as_text()).map(String::from))\n1192\t            .collect();\n1193\t\n1194\t        assert!(tables.contains(&\"issues\".to_string()));\n1195\t        assert!(tables.contains(&\"dependencies\".to_string()));\n1196\t        assert!(tables.contains(&\"config\".to_string()));\n1197\t        assert!(tables.contains(&\"dirty_issues\".to_string()));\n1198\t\n1199\t        // Verify pragmas\n1200\t        let row = conn.query_row(\"PRAGMA journal_mode\").unwrap();\n1201\t        let journal_mode = row\n1202\t            .get(0)\n1203\t            .and_then(|v| v.as_text())\n1204\t            .unwrap_or(\"\")\n1205\t            .to_string();\n1206\t        // In-memory DBs use MEMORY journaling, regardless of what we set\n1207\t        assert!(journal_mode.to_uppercase() == \"WAL\" || journal_mode.to_uppercase() == \"MEMORY\");\n1208\t\n1209\t        let row = conn.query_row(\"PRAGMA foreign_keys\").unwrap();\n1210\t        let foreign_keys = row.get(0).and_then(SqliteValue::as_integer).unwrap_or(0);\n1211\t        assert_eq!(foreign_keys, 1);\n1212\t    }\n1213\t\n1214\t    #[test]\n1215\t    fn test_apply_schema_file_backed_has_no_duplicate_issues_columns() {\n1216\t        let temp = TempDir::new().expect(\"tempdir\");\n1217\t        let db_path = temp.path().join(\"beads.db\");\n1218\t        let conn = Connection::open(db_path.to_string_lossy().into_owned()).unwrap();\n1219\t\n1220\t        apply_schema(&conn).expect(\"Failed to apply schema\");\n1221\t\n1222\t        let row = conn\n1223\t            .query_row(\"SELECT sql FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='issues'\")\n1224\t            .expect(\"issues table should exist\");\n1225\t        let issues_sql = row\n1226\t            .get(0)\n1227\t            .and_then(SqliteValue::as_text)\n1228\t            .expect(\"issues table SQL should be present\");\n1229\t\n1230\t        assert_eq!(\n1231\t            issues_sql.matches(\"source_repo\").count(),\n1232\t            1,\n1233\t            \"issues table SQL should define source_repo exactly once\"\n1234\t        );\n1235\t        assert_eq!(\n1236\t            issues_sql.matches(\"is_template\").count(),\n1237\t            1,\n1238\t            \"issues table SQL should define is_template exactly once\"\n1239\t        );\n1240\t    }\n1241\t\n1242\t    /// Conformance test: Verify schema matches bd (Go) for interoperability.\n1243\t    /// Tests table structure, defaults, constraints, and indexes.\n1244\t    #[test]\n1245\t    #[allow(clippy::too_many_lines)]\n1246\t    fn test_schema_parity_conformance() {\n1247\t        let conn = .first()\n1820\t            .and_then(|row| row.get(0).and_then(SqliteValue::as_integer))\n1821\t            .unwrap_or(0);\n1822\t        assert_eq!(config_key_pk, 0);\n1823\t\n1824\t        let .and_then(|row| row.get(0).and_then(SqliteValue::as_integer))\n1829\t            .unwrap_or(0);\n1830\t        assert_eq!(metadata_key_pk, 0);\n1831\t\n1832\t        // Migration should preserve existing values.\n1833\t        let config_latest = conn\n1834\t            .query_row_with_params(\n1835\t                \"SELECT value FROM config WHERE key = ?\",\n1836\t                &[SqliteValue::from(\"issue_prefix\")],\n1837\t            )\n1838\t            .unwrap();\n1839\t        assert_eq!(\n1840\t            config_latest.get(0).and_then(SqliteValue::as_text),\n1841\t            Some(\"new\")\n1842\t        );\n1843\t\n1844\t        let metadata_latest = conn\n1845\t            .query_row_with_params(\n1846\t                \"SELECT value FROM metadata WHERE key = ?\",\n1847\t                &[SqliteValue::from(\"project\")],\n1848\t            )\n1849\t            .unwrap();\n1850\t        assert_eq!(\n1851\t            metadata_latest.get(0).and_then(SqliteValue::as_text),\n1852\t            Some(\"new\")\n1853\t        );\n1854\t    }\n1855\t\n1856\t    #[test]\n1857\t    fn
